goal: Convert open-loop continuation into reference-anchored, trace-informed closed-loop continuation during disconnect.
design_principle: trace feedback must be applied relative to orientation reference, not as raw state injection

changes.location: inside main loop, replace phi_for_internal logic
changes.old_code: phi_for_internal = phi_oriented_actual if connected else (pending_phi_continued if pending_phi_continued is not None else phi_oriented_actual)
changes.new_code:
if connected:
    phi_for_internal = phi_oriented_actual
else:
    base = pending_phi_continued if pending_phi_continued is not None else phi_oriented_actual

    # Trace feedback (directional, not absolute)
    trace_vec = phase_continuation.trace_feedback_vector()

    # Inductive bias (already reference-aligned)
    inductive_vec = phi_inductive

    # Weak global anchor (prevents full drift of reference)
    anchor_vec = prev_phi_oriented if prev_phi_oriented is not None else base

    # Blend weights (tunable)
    w_base = 1.0
    w_trace = 0.35 if trace_vec is not None else 0.0
    w_inductive = 0.25
    w_anchor = 0.15

    blended = w_base * base

    if trace_vec is not None:
        blended += w_trace * trace_vec

    if inductive_vec is not None:
        blended += w_inductive * inductive_vec

    blended += w_anchor * anchor_vec

    # Normalize to maintain admissibility
    phi_for_internal = blended / (np.linalg.norm(blended) + 1e-9)

changes.note: no structural rewrite needed; ensure it returns normalized directional vector only (already true)

changes.addition:
# Optional damping to prevent runaway drift
if not connected:
    damping = 0.92
    phi_for_internal = damping * phi_for_internal + (1.0 - damping) * (prev_phi_oriented if prev_phi_oriented is not None else phi_for_internal)
    phi_for_internal = phi_for_internal / (np.linalg.norm(phi_for_internal) + 1e-9)

expected_effect: anchors orientation reference -(i) weakly without freezing

interpretation.if_no_change: trace not strong enough or anchor misaligned

note: This patch implements reference-relative memory feedback, converting continuation from open-loop prediction to constrained dynamical persistence aligned with -(i).
